          "text": "If you're looking to enhance your photography skills through online courses in the UK, here are some reputable options to consider:

**Introduction to Photography (Online) by Teesside University**  
A 12-week, 100% online course designed for beginners and those looking to expand their skills. It covers technical aspects like ISO, aperture, and shutter speed, as well as compositional techniques such as the rule of thirds and leading lines. The course includes live, tutor-led sessions and weekly hands-on tasks. ([tees.ac.uk](https://www.tees.ac.uk/parttime_courses/art_%26_design/ucce_introduction_to_photography_%28online%29.cfm?utm_source=openai))

**Master Your Photography**  
Offered by award-winning photographers Paul and Kate Cooper, this platform provides practical, jargon-free online courses suitable for all levels. Courses include 'Camera Kickstart' for beginners and 'Master Your Mobile' for smartphone photography enthusiasts. The courses are designed to help you take sharp, confident photos. ([masteryour.photography](https://masteryour.photography/?utm_source=openai))



[Best online photography courses 2021 - beginners to advanced](https://www.whatsthebest.co.uk/tech/photography/best-online-photography-courses/?utm_source=openai)
**Institute of Photography**  
Established in 2012, the Institute offers a range of online photography courses, including the 'Diploma in Professional Photography' and the 'Diploma in Photography.' These courses are ideal for those serious about advancing their photography skills, with in-depth modules and assignments. ([institute-of-photography.com](https://www.institute-of-photography.com/?utm_source=openai))

[
  {
    "platform": "google",
    "model_name": "google_ai_overview",
    "location_code": 2826,
    "language_code": "en",
    "question": "macro shots",
    "answer": "Macro shots are `extreme close-up photographs that reveal fine details of small subjects, making them appear larger than life on the camera sensor, often at a 1:1 life-size ratio or greater`.This photographic technique is used to showcase intricate details of subjects like insects, flowers, and other natural elements, bringing hidden aspects of the world to the forefront.[[1]](https://www.adobe.com/uk/creativecloud/photography/discover/macro-photography.html#:~:text=Macro%20photography%20is%20all%20about,or%20photos%20of%20the%20microscopic.%29)[[2]](https://www.masterclass.com/articles/complete-macro-photography-guide-for-beginners#:~:text=a%20Better%20Photographer?-,What%20Is%20Macro%20Photography?,image%20of%20a%20small%20subject.)[[3]](https://www.canon.co.uk/get-inspired/tips-and-techniques/macro-photography-tips/#:~:text=For%20effective%20macro%20photography%2C%20you,is%20always%20something%20to%20shoot.)

You can watch this video to see examples of macro photography and learn about the techniques involved:

[![](https://api.dataforseo.com/cdn/i/09080343-1087-0066-0000-62d7c2d63d41:1)
Ben Harvey Photography
YouTube · 28 Jul 2024](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nZo7RbTp4lQ&t=718)

Key Characteristics of Macro Shots:

- **Extreme Close-ups:** The primary goal is to get as close to the subject as possible.[[1]](https://www.adobe.com/uk/creativecloud/photography/discover/macro-photography.html#:~:text=Macro%20photography%20is%20all%20about,or%20photos%20of%20the%20microscopic.%29)
- **Magnification:** Subjects are magnified to a scale that is the same size or larger than their actual dimensions on the camera's sensor.[[1]](https://www.masterclass.com/articles/complete-macro-photography-guide-for-beginners#:~:text=a%20Better%20Photographer?-,What%20Is%20Macro%20Photography?,image%20of%20a%20small%20subject.)[[2]](https://www.alanranger.com/blog-on-photography/art-of-macro-photography#:~:text=Macro%20Photography%20Definition,reproduced%20on%20the%20camera%20sensor.)
- **Focus on Detail:** The emphasis is on the fine details, textures, and intricate features of small subjects that are often overlooked in normal-sized photographs.[[1]](https://www.adobe.com/uk/creativecloud/photography/discover/macro-photography.html#:~:text=Macro%20photography%20is%20all%20about,or%20photos%2
…[truncated 131317 chars]

    "question": "did photos exist in the 1700s?",
    "answer": "No. If by \"photos\" you mean photographs created by a camera and permanently recorded, they did **not exist in the 1700s**.\n\nPeople in the 1700s did have devices such as the camera obscura, which could project a real-world scene onto a surface. Artists sometimes used these projections to help with drawing. However, there was no practical way to permanently capture the image automatically. [www.britannica.com](https://www.britannica.com/question/Who-invented-the-photograph-and-why?utm_source=chatgpt.com)\n\nThe first permanent photograph is generally credited to Nicéphore Niépce, who created an image known as *View from the Window at Le Gras* in 1826 or 1827 using a process called heliography. The exposure took many hours. [www.britannica.com](https://www.britannica.com/technology/photography?utm_source=chatgpt.com)\n\nPhotography became practical a little later when Louis Daguerre developed the daguerreotype process, which was publicly announced in 1839 and dramatically reduced exposure times. [www.britannica.com](https://www.britannica.com/topic/12-Key-Dates-in-the-History-of-Photography?utm_source=chatgpt.com)\n\nSo:\n- **1700s:** No photographs, but camera obscuras existed. [www.britannica.com](https://www.britannica.com/question/Who-invented-the-photograph-and-why?utm_source=chatgpt.com)\n- **1826–1827:** First permanent photograph. [www.britannica.com](https://www.britannica.com/technology/photography?utm_source=chatgpt.com)\n- **1839:** Photography becomes practical and begins to spread. [www.britannica.com](https://www.britannica.com/topic/12-Key-Dates-in-the-History-of-Photography?utm_source=chatgpt.com)\n\nIf you're wondering whether a specific image claimed to be from the 1700s could be real, I can help evaluate it.",

Filmed in Australia and New Zealand, the gritty survival movie focuses on intense, R-rated tactical combat as a small, injured squad tries to survive a, at first, seemingly unstoppable enemy.[[1]](https://www.imdb.com/title/tt15940132/)[[2]](https://www.youtube.com/shorts/wY_JEBSWeaw)[[3]](https://www.empireonline.com/movies/reviews/war-machine-2026/)[[4]](https://www.netflix.com/tudum/articles/war-machine-release-date)[[5]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/War_Machine_%282026_film%29)

**Key Details** 

- **Release Date:** March 2026 (Netflix)
- **Director:** Patrick Hughes (*The Expendables 3*, *Hitman's Bodyguard*)
- **Starring:** Alan Ritchson ("81"), Dennis Quaid (Sheridan), Stephan James, and Jai Courtney
- **Genre:** Sci-Fi Action / Survival Horror
- **Plot:** During a final, high-altitude training exercise, a team of Ranger recruits, led by a soldier haunted by the death of his brother in Afghanistan, faces an alien threat, forcing them to fight for survival.

    "question": "what is paul hart known for?",
    "answer": "
It depends a bit which **“Paul Hart”** you mean — there are a few prominent people with that name. Here are two of the best known, and what they’re known for.  

---

## ⚽ Paul Hart (born 1953) — Footballer & Coach  
- He was a professional English football **defender**, with a long playing career from 1970–1988. In total he made **567 league appearances** playing for clubs such as Blackpool, Leeds United, Nottingham Forest and Sheffield Wednesday. [en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Paul_Hart?utm_source=chatgpt.com)[www.skysports.com](https://www.skysports.com/football/news/11715/9977444/paul-hart-is-the-new-leeds-united-academy-director?utm_source=chatgpt.com)  
- After retiring as a player, he transitioned into coaching and management — he’s held managerial or coaching roles with many clubs (e.g. as manager of Nottingham Forest, Barnsley, Crystal Palace as caretaker, Portsmouth, Queens Park Rangers, and youth/academy roles at various clubs). [en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Paul_Hart?utm_source=chatgpt.com)  
- He’s also been recognized for his work in youth development. For example — during his time with Leeds United’s academy — he helped bring through talents such as Harry Kewell, Alan Smith, Jonathan Woodgate, and others. [en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Paul_Hart?utm_source=chatgpt.com)[www.skysports.com](https://www.skysports.com/football/news/11715/9977444/paul-hart-is-the-new-leeds-united-academy-director?utm_source=chatgpt.com)  
- More recently (as of 2024), he was appointed Assistant Manager at Charlton Athletic under manager Nathan Jones. [www.charltonafc.com](https://www.charltonafc.com/news/paul-hart-joins-assistant-manager?utm_source=chatgpt.com)  

In short: this Paul Hart is known for a long and accomplished career in English football — as a dependable defender, and then as a coach/manager deeply involved in developing younger players.

---

## 📸 Paul Hart (born 1961) — Photographer  
- This Paul Hart is a British **landscape photographer**, acclaimed for his black-and-white analogue photography. He works with medium- and large-format film cameras, processes and prints his own images in a darkroom. [en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Paul_Hart_%28photographer%29?utm_source=chatgpt.com)
